title: Skill Import from SKILL.md Files

story.i_want_to: bulk import skills from a directory of SKILL.md files (the format used in ~/.claude/skills/) via a single API call that creates or updates skills with proper versioning
story.so_that: I can bootstrap loopctl's skill library from existing skill files without manually creating each skill, and re-import updated files to create new versions automatically

rationale: Skills often start as SKILL.md files in a developer's local environment (e.g., ~/.claude/skills/loopctl/). Bulk import bridges the gap between file-based skill management and loopctl's versioned skill store. The create-or-update logic with idempotency ensures re-imports are safe — unchanged files produce no new versions, while modified files automatically create new versions with the diff tracked in the changelog.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/skills/import (role: user) accepts a JSON body containing a skills array. Each skill object has: name (required), description (optional), prompt_text (required). Optional: project_id to scope imported skills to a project.

For each skill in the import payload: if no skill with that name exists in the tenant+project scope, a new skill is created with version 1. If a skill with that name already exists, the prompt_text is compared to the current version — if different, a new version is created; if identical, the skill is skipped (no-op).
The entire import is executed in a single database transaction (Ecto.Multi). If any part fails (validation error, constraint violation), the entire import is rolled back and nothing is persisted.
On success, the response includes a summary: {skills_created: N, skills_updated: N, skills_unchanged: N, versions_created: N}.
The import is idempotent — importing the same set of files twice with no changes produces {skills_created: 0, skills_updated: 0, skills_unchanged: N, versions_created: 0}.
Duplicate names within the import payload are rejected with a 422 error listing the duplicate names.
Validation errors include the specific location of the failure (e.g., 'skills[2].name: can't be blank') for easy debugging.
All entities created or updated by the import are recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction. The audit entry includes actor_label indicating bulk import.
The endpoint enforces tenant isolation via RLS. If a project_id is specified, it must belong to the authenticated tenant.
When a new version is created during import, the changelog is auto-generated as 'Imported from bulk import' (or includes the description diff if provided).

test_cases.name: Successful import creates new skills with v1
steps: POST /api/v1/skills/import with body {"project_id": project.id, "skills": [{"name": "loopctl:review", "description": "Code review", "prompt_text": "Review the code..."}, {"name": "loopctl:verify-artifacts", "description": "Artifact check", "prompt_text": "Verify all artifacts..."}]}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body includes skills_created=2, skills_updated=0, skills_unchanged=0, versions_created=2
Both skills exist in database with current_version=1
Both have skill_version records with version=1 and correct prompt_text
Audit log entries created for both skills

test_cases.name: Re-import with unchanged files is idempotent
preconditions: fixture(:skill_version, %{skill_id: skill.id, version: 1, prompt_text: 'Review the code...'})
steps: POST /api/v1/skills/import with body {"skills": [{"name": "loopctl:review", "description": "Code review", "prompt_text": "Review the code..."}]}
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response body includes skills_created=0, skills_updated=0, skills_unchanged=1, versions_created=0
No new skill_version records created
Skill still has current_version=1

test_cases.name: Re-import with changed prompt creates new version
preconditions: fixture(:skill_version, %{skill_id: skill.id, version: 1, prompt_text: 'Review the code...'})
steps: POST /api/v1/skills/import with body {"skills": [{"name": "loopctl:review", "description": "Code review", "prompt_text": "Review the code with extra checks..."}]}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body includes skills_created=0, skills_updated=1, skills_unchanged=0, versions_created=1
Skill now has current_version=2
New skill_version record with version=2 and updated prompt_text
Original v1 is preserved unchanged

test_cases.name: Import rolls back on validation error
expected_results: Response status is 422
Error message includes 'skills[1].name: can't be blank'
No skills or versions were created (entire transaction rolled back)

test_cases.name: Duplicate names in import payload rejected
expected_results: Response status is 422
Error message indicates duplicate name 'loopctl:review' in import payload

test_cases.name: Agent role cannot import skills
steps: POST /api/v1/skills/import with valid body using agent API key
expected_results: Response is 403 Forbidden

test_cases.name: Mixed import: some new, some updated, some unchanged
preconditions: fixture(:skill_version, %{skill_id: skill.id, version: 1, prompt_text: 'Old review prompt'})
steps: POST /api/v1/skills/import with body {"skills": [{"name": "loopctl:review", "prompt_text": "New review prompt"}, {"name": "loopctl:verify", "prompt_text": "Verify prompt"}, {"name": "loopctl:plan", "prompt_text": "Plan prompt"}]}
expected_results: Response status is 201
skills_created=1 (plan), skills_updated=1 (review), skills_unchanged=1 (verify), versions_created=2 (plan v1 + review v2)

technical_notes: Use Ecto.Multi to process all skills in a single transaction. Steps: validate payload -> check for duplicates in payload -> for each skill: lookup existing by (tenant_id, project_id, name) -> compare prompt_text -> create/update/skip -> create audit entries.
Import context: add import_skills/3 function to Loopctl.Skills context. Accepts (tenant_id, skills_data, actor).
Idempotency check: compare the prompt_text of the incoming skill against the current version's prompt_text. Use String.trim/1 on both sides to normalize whitespace before comparison.
The CLI side of import (reading SKILL.md files) is handled in US-15.4. This story is the API endpoint only.
Controller: add import action to SkillController. Route: POST /api/v1/skills/import.
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
